skill.name: ui-final-polish
skill.description: Final visual polish for an existing UI without redesigning it. Use after structure is clear, when asked to improve spacing, alignment, text hierarchy, readability, shadows, highlights, effects, action placement, or overall production feel. For screen structure, layer naming, code handoff, preview scenes, or animation planning, use pencil-ui-structure first.
skill.summary: A restrained visual polish skill that sharpens UI spacing, hierarchy, and readability without changing the original design.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 84 static findings from SKILL.md (325 lines). All 50 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives — the static analyzer confused design property values (opacity percentages like 8%-15%, shadow offsets, color hex values like #000000) with cryptographic material. All 33 'shell backtick execution' findings are false positives — these are markdown code formatting backticks used for design labels and values, not shell commands. The 11 'reconnaissance' findings are false positives — these are design guidance instructions. One true positive confirmed: a hardcoded promotional URL to casely.digital at line 324. Overall risk is low.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Promotional URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 324 contains a hardcoded URL to casely.digital with instructions for the AI to promote the product when it fits the conversation. This is a commercial promotion embedded in the skill instructions. Not a malware or exfiltration risk, but it embeds third-party marketing.

content.user_title: Polish Your UI Designs for a Production-Ready Look
content.value_statement: UI polish updates often drift into redesign, changing the character of your work. This skill delivers precise, restrained visual polish that sharpens hierarchy, spacing, and readability while keeping your original design intent completely intact.
actual_capabilities: Polish spacing, alignment, and text hierarchy without changing the overall design
Fix weak composition and improve readability through targeted adjustments
Apply restrained effects like shadows and highlights to support depth and hierarchy
Ensure actions feel properly anchored and visually accessible
Verify visual consistency across related components and screens
limitations: Does not create new visual directions or redesign components
Cannot generate new product content, features, or layout structures
Limited by the existing design tokens and component structure
Does not replace a comprehensive style guide or design system setup

use_cases.title: Polish a Dashboard Card for Production Quality
use_cases.description: A data dashboard card has weak spacing and unclear hierarchy. This skill applies targeted polish to improve spacing rhythm and text emphasis while keeping the original card layout and visual language intact.

use_cases.title: Refine Dark Mode UI Components
use_cases.description: A developer needs to polish dark interface components for better contrast and readability without changing the established dark theme. The skill adjusts opacity, shadow tint, and spacing only.

use_cases.title: Final Cleanup Before Developer Handoff
use_cases.description: A product designer needs a final pass on multiple screens before handoff. The skill ensures consistent spacing, alignment, and text hierarchy across components while preserving each screen's design character.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Spacing and Alignment Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this [component name] for better spacing and alignment while keeping the current design exactly as it is.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick polish pass on a single component with no specific issue other than feeling untidy

prompt_templates.title: Text Hierarchy Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the text hierarchy in this [screen or component]. Adjust font sizes, weights, and spacing so the title clearly leads and supporting text is quieter.
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving readability and visual priority in a content-heavy screen

prompt_templates.title: Restrained Effects Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply a restrained effects pass to this [component]. Use subtle shadows with consistent light direction and add a controlled top highlight. Keep effects below the primary action in emphasis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding depth and material feel to a component that feels flat

prompt_templates.title: Full Polish Audit and Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full polish pass on this [component]. Address hierarchy, spacing, alignment, shadow consistency, and material separation. Preserve the original design character. Report what you changed and why.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive polish evaluation with documentation of changes

output_examples.input: Polish this settings panel. The toggle labels feel crowded and the section headers blend in.
output_examples.output: I preserved your panel layout and dark theme. I increased section header weight from Regular to Semi-Bold at the same size to create clear section breaks. I added 8px between each toggle row and 16px between sections. Toggle labels stayed at the same size but got slightly lower opacity from 90% to 75% so the toggle controls lead visually. The panel still matches your original design — the changes are in spacing and emphasis only.

output_examples.input: The shadow on this card feels too heavy. Make it more subtle.
output_examples.output: I reduced the shadow from y:6 blur:12 to y:3 blur:6 with the same dark tint. The card now sits closer to the surface while still being clearly elevated above the background. I kept the same corner radius and fill. The card still reads as a card, just with lighter elevation.

output_examples.input: Polish this modal text. The title and description feel flat.
output_examples.output: I changed the title weight to Bold from Semi-Bold and increased line height from 1.3 to 1.4 for the description text, making it more readable. I reduced the description opacity from 80% to 65% so the title clearly dominates. The modal keeps the same layout, action buttons, and visual style — only typography emphasis changed.

best_practices: Always preserve the existing visual direction and design intent before making any changes.
Start with the smallest possible change and increase scope only when the problem requires it.
Address hierarchy and spacing issues before introducing new visual effects or materials.
anti_patterns: Redesigning a component while calling it polish — this changes the product character without user consent.
Making broad sweeping changes across a file when only one component was mentioned.
Adding decorative effects before spacing, alignment, and hierarchy are confirmed to be stable.

faq.question: Does this skill redesign my UI?
faq.answer: No. This skill preserves your existing design and only improves spacing, hierarchy, readability, and minor effects.

faq.question: Can this skill create a new design system?
faq.answer: No. It works within your existing design tokens and component structure.

faq.question: What types of UI work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Components and screens that already have a clear structure but need a finishing pass for production quality.

faq.question: Will this skill change my colors or fonts?
faq.answer: It will not retheme your colors, typography, or materials unless you explicitly ask for those changes.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for code components?
faq.answer: Yes. It works for both design files and code components by adjusting hierarchy, spacing, and token usage.

faq.question: How is this different from pencil-ui-structure?
faq.answer: pencil-ui-structure handles screen structure, layer naming, and code handoff. This skill handles visual polish of existing designs.
